subject: Geography
chapter: Solar Radiation, Heat Balance and Temperature

remember: Students will be able to recall the factors affecting solar radiation.
Students will be able to explain the concept of heat balance and temperature.
Students will be able to describe the distribution of solar radiation on the Earth's surface.
understand: Students will be able to understand the importance of solar radiation in the Earth's climate system.
Students will be able to explain the concept of heat transfer and its role in shaping the Earth's temperature.
Students will be able to describe the factors influencing the formation of regional climate.
apply: Students will be able to apply the knowledge of solar radiation and heat balance to explain temperature variations in different regions.
Students will be able to use the concept of heat transfer to explain the formation of weather patterns.
Students will be able to analyze the impact of human activities on the Earth's climate system.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the relationship between solar radiation and the Earth's temperature.
Students will be able to evaluate the role of heat balance in shaping the Earth's climate.
Students will be able to compare the temperature variations in different regions of the world.

ncert_objectives: Explain the distribution of solar radiation on the Earth's surface.
Describe the factors affecting solar radiation and heat balance.
Discuss the importance of temperature in the Earth's climate system.
board_exam_objectives: To test the understanding of factors affecting solar radiation and heat balance.
To assess the knowledge of temperature variations in different regions of the world.
To evaluate the impact of human activities on the Earth's climate system.
